jira_detail.summary: [ASYS]: test_import_export.py :Expected (Pool) but not found in exported_config
jira_detail.description:
*Summary*
*test_import_export.py* :Expected (Pool) but not found in exported_config .

{*}Steps to reproduce{*}:
The test initiated an export of a single Virtual Service, test-virtualservice-1
The export API (/api/configuration/export/virtualservice/) processed the request.
The API failed to include the VS's dependent Pool object in the exported configuration data.
The test's verification step checked the exported data for the presence of the Pool object. 
The verification failed with a RuntimeError because the Pool key was missing, causing the test case to fail.

 

{*}Snapshot from apiserver.INFO{*}:
{code:java}
2026-04-09T00:27:07.657Z	I  10386  	[T-ID=21a5dd05]	configurationtool/config_utils.go:288	Exporter Initalizing took 596.302137ms
2026-04-09T00:27:07.666Z	I  44148  	[T-ID=21a5dd05]	configurationtool/registry.go:17	Running ExportCustomRoutines for Pool
2026-04-09T00:27:07.666Z	I  44148  	[T-ID=21a5dd05]	configurationtool/registry.go:17	Running ExportCustomRoutines for VirtualService
2026-04-09T00:27:07.667Z	I  44148  	[T-ID=21a5dd05]	configurationtool/config_utils.go:288	ConfigurationExport took 9.367688ms
2026-04-09T00:27:07.669Z	I  44148  	alert/config_log.go:303	[Config] EventId: CONFIG_EXPORT {code}
The apiserver.INFO log shows that at the time of the test failure, the controller was actively processing a configuration export request. The logs indicate that routines for both Pool and VirtualService were executed. However, despite the Pool routine running, the final exported config failed to include the pool-1 object, pointing to a flaw in the dependency collection logic.

{*}Platforms{*}: Vmware-write-access , Vmware-write-access-fips
